'''Brock Greacen''' (born 18 June 2002) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} forward for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Born in [[Denman, New South Wales]], Greacen played his junior rugby league for the [[Denman Devils]],<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://footyfortress.com/Player-Profile/Profiles/PlayerProfile145.html|title=The Knights Realm Player Profile|website=footyfortress.com}}</ref> before being signed by the [[Newcastle Knights]].

==Playing career==

===Early years===
Greacen rose through the ranks for the Newcastle Knights, playing his first game with their [[NSW Cup]] team in 2022.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTIxMy0yMjYzLDI3OTktLS0tLS0tMzU2NTMtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List - RLP}}</ref>

===2025===
In 2025, Greacen signed a train and trial contract with the Knights' [[National Rugby League|NRL]] team.<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2025-early-mail-late-mail-round-15-ins-and-outs-players-cut-changes-injuries-david-fifita-sam-verrills-titans-vs-sea-eagles/news-story/9cab5e76a05d9ce76db1a330ca9f31a6|title=Horror Knights blow as key flyer sidelined; Luckless Roosters star's fresh injury — NRL Late Mail|date=13 June 2025|website=Fox Sports}}</ref> In round 15 of the [[2025 NRL season]], he made his NRL debut for the Knights against the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2025/06/13/nrl-squad-update-round-15/|title=NRL Squad Update: Round 15|date=13 June 2025|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref><ref name="auto"/>

On 31 July 2025, [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Greacen on a two-year deal from the 2026 season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=31 July 2025 |title=Castleford sign prop Greacen on two-year deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cp94n01vv48o |access-date=31 July 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=31 July 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers swoop for NRL star as recruitment drives hots up |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-swoop-for-nrl-star-as-recruitment-drives-hots-up |access-date=31 July 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

=== 2026 ===
Greacen was assigned Castleford's number 14 shirt for the [[2026 Castleford Tigers season|2026 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=26 December 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers reveal 2026 squad numbers with new-look top 17 and shocks confirmed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-reveal-2026-squad-numbers-with-new-look-top-17-and-shocks-confirmed |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He made his debut on 7 February against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the [[2026 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=7 February 2026 |title=Doncaster 0 Castleford Tigers 14: Not picture perfect but job done for Ryan Carr |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/doncaster-0-castleford-tigers-14-not-picture-perfect-but-job-done-for-ryan-carr-5507339 |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> however picked up a knock and was ruled out for four weeks.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Jackson |first=Will |date=26 February 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers star suffers broken leg as Ryan Carr gives updates on Brock Greacen and Semi Valemei |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/castleford-tigers-star-suffers-broken-leg-as-ryan-carr-gives-updates-on-brock-greacen-and-semi-valemei-113059/ |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=13 March 2026 |title=Why Semi Valemei won't debut for Castleford Tigers plus latest on Brock Greacen |url=https://www.totalrl.com/why-semi-valemei-wont-debut-for-castleford-tigers-plus-latest-on-brock-greacen/ |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> He scored his first try for the club in round 11 against [[York Knights]], holding off a number of defenders to touch down for the winning try,<ref>{{Cite web |date=16 May 2026 |title=Castleford edge out York in rollercoaster encounter |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cr7p429k4lmo |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Craven |first=Dave |date=16 May 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers edge York Knights |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-edge-york-knights/ |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> and was named in the team of the week.<ref>{{Cite web |date=18 May 2026 |title=Round 11 Team of the Week |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/6087/round-11-team-of-the-week |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=Super League}}</ref> The following round, he raced through a gap to score again in Castleford's victory against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=24 May 2026 |title=Qareqare wings in for hat-trick as Castleford Tigers dominate against St Helens |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/qareqare-wings-in-for-hat-trick-as-castleford-tigers-dominate-against-st-helens-8638641 |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=Wakefield
